text: Continuous Integration, often called CI, is a software development practice that helps teams build better code. In CI, developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Every time this happens, a CI pipeline starts automatically. First, it performs an automated build to see if the new code compiles correctly. Then, it runs a set of tests, such as unit and integration tests. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. This saves time and reduces frustration. As a result, the team can be more confident that the new code works well with the rest of the project.
